text.title: ops-tax-compliance Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ops-tax-compliance as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: LLMs cite tax rules from prior years as current-year law.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always require the agent to cite an IRS publication number and revision date. Standard deduction, mileage rate, HSA/401(k) limits change annually. Verify against IRS.gov before acting.

failure-mode.description: Agents produce confident-sounding state nexus conclusions for jurisdictions the business has never operated in.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: State nexus rules vary on dozens of dimensions (economic nexus thresholds, affiliate nexus, click-through nexus). Agents flatten this to single-rule reasoning. Require a CPA for any new-state nexus determination.

failure-mode.description: Agent pastes full tax IDs, SSNs, EINs, or bank account numbers into prompts.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Any data in the prompt can appear in logs, context summaries, or API response metadata. Use a scrubber pipeline before any LLM call involving financial records.

failure-mode.description: Agent drafts a response to an IRS notice without mandatory CPA sign-off.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: IRS-facing communications trigger formal processes. An incorrect response can waive defenses or create new liability. Agents should only prepare a facts-and-timeline summary for the human and CPA to review.
